Reuses the build +\\n# health + token path proven by run-sim-local.sh and the fixture from\\n# run-sim-ble-match.sh.\\n#\\n# Usage:  ./scripts/run-sim-identity.sh\\n# Env:    SIM_NODES, SIM_WALKERS, SIM_RATE, SIM_DURATION, SIM_SEED, SIM_PORT,\\n#         SIM_SPACE, SIM_WALKER_MAC, SIM_PERSON_NAME, SIM_PERSON_COLOR,\\n#         SPAXEL_MOTHERSHIP_BIN, SPAXEL_SIM_BIN\\n# Exit:   0 if a live /api/blobs blob carries non-empty canonical identity, 1 otherwise.\\n#\\n# Requires: go, curl, jq.\\n\\nset -uo pipefail\\n\\nROOT=\\\"$(cd \\\"$(dirname \\\"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\\\")/..\\\" && pwd)\\\"\\nMS=\\\"${SPAXEL_MOTHERSHIP_BIN:-/tmp/spaxel-mothership}\\\"\\nSIM=\\\"${SPAXEL_SIM_BIN:-/tmp/spaxel-sim}\\\"\\nPORT=\\\"${SIM_PORT:-8088}\\\"\\n\\n# walkers=1: one registered person (Alice) -> one identity-bearing blob is the\\n# cleanest evidence. rate=30 is REQUIRED for deterministic blob emission\\n# (hardware-free-runtime.md gotcha #2). seed=42 pins walker-0 to advertise\\n# AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:00 (cmd/sim/main.go: fmt AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:%02X, IDs from 0).\\nSIM_NODES=\\\"${SIM_NODES:-4}\\\"\\nSIM_WALKERS=\\\"${SIM_WALKERS:-1}\\\"\\nSIM_RATE=\\\"${SIM_RATE:-30}\\\"\\nSIM_DURATION=\\\"${SIM_DURATION:-25}\\\"\\nSIM_SEED=\\\"${SIM_SEED:-42}\\\"\\nSIM_SPACE=\\\"${SIM_SPACE:-5x5x2.5}\\\"\\n\\nWALKER_MAC=\\\"${SIM_WALKER_MAC:-AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:00}\\\"\\nPERSON_NAME=\\\"${SIM_PERSON_NAME:-Alice}\\\"\\nPERSON_COLOR=\\\"${SIM_PERSON_COLOR:-#4488ff}\\\"\\n\\nlog() { printf '[run-sim-id] %s\\\\n' \\\"$*\\\"; }\\ndie() { printf '[run-sim-id] ERROR: %s\\\\n' \\\"$*\\\" >&2; exit 1; }\\n\\nfor dep in go curl jq; do\\n  command -v \\\"$dep\\\" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die \\\"missing required dependency: $dep\\\"\\ndone\\n\\n# --- Build both binaries from source (reuse if present; force sim has --ble) ----\\n# Same canonical-source rule as run-sim-local.sh: build ONLY mothership/cmd/sim\\n# (it has --ble/--verify); never the repo-root cmd/sim/ (older, lacks them).\\nlog \\\"building canonical binaries from source (reuse if present)...\\\"\\nneed_build=0\\n[ -x \\\"$MS\\\" ]  || need_build=1\\n[ -x \\\"$SIM\\\" ] || need_build=1\\nif [ -x \\\"$SIM\\\" ] && ! \\\"$SIM\\\" --help 2>&1 | grep -q -- '--ble'; then\\n  log \\\"existing $SIM lacks --ble (stale/standalone build); rebuilding...\\\"\\n  rm -f \\\"$SIM\\\"; need_build=1\\nfi\\nif [ \\\"$need_build\\\" -eq 1 ]; then\\n  ( cd \\\"$ROOT/mothership\\\" || exit 1\\n    go build -o \\\"$MS\\\"  ./cmd/mothership || die \\\"building mothership\\\"\\n    go build -o \\\"$SIM\\\" ./cmd/sim         || die \\\"building sim\\\"\\n  ) || exit 1\\nfi\\n\\nDATA_DIR=$(mktemp -d -t spaxel-runsimid-data-XXXXXX)\\nMS_PID=\\\"\\\"\\nSIM_PID=\\\"\\\"\\n\\ncleanup() {\\n  [ -n \\\"$SIM_PID\\\" ] && kill \\\"$SIM_PID\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n  [ -n \\\"$MS_PID\\\" ]  && kill -INT \\\"$MS_PID\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n  sleep 1\\n  [ -n \\\"$MS_PID\\\" ]  && kill -9 \\\"$MS_PID\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n  wait 2>/dev/null || true\\n  # CAPTURE_DIR hook (bf-15oi): if set, persist the full log artifact\\n  # (mothership.log, sim.log, identity_blob.json) to a durable location before\\n  # the temp dir is removed, so the run can be scanned/referenced after exit.\\n  # Purely additive — unset (the default) preserves the prior cleanup behaviour.\\n  if [ -n \\\"${CAPTURE_DIR:-}\\\" ]; then\\n    mkdir -p \\\"$CAPTURE_DIR\\\"\\n    cp -a \\\"$DATA_DIR\\\"/. \\\"$CAPTURE_DIR\\\"/ 2>/dev/null || true\\n  fi\\n  rm -rf \\\"$DATA_DIR\\\"\\n}\\ntrap cleanup EXIT INT TERM\\n\\nlog \\\"mothership: $MS\\\"\\nlog \\\"sim:        $SIM\\\"\\nlog \\\"params: nodes=$SIM_NODES walkers=$SIM_WALKERS rate=$SIM_RATE duration=${SIM_DURATION}s seed=$SIM_SEED space=$SIM_SPACE port=$PORT ble=on\\\"\\nlog \\\"identity fixture: $WALKER_MAC -> person \\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\" ($PERSON_COLOR)\\\"\\n\\n# --- Start mothership on an ephemeral data dir (identical env to run-sim-ble-match) --\\n# SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=info surfaces matcher/fleet INFO lines for evidence capture.\\nSPAXEL_BIND_ADDR=\\\"127.0.0.1:$PORT\\\" \\\\\\nSPAXEL_DATA_DIR=\\\"$DATA_DIR\\\" \\\\\\nSPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=info \\\\\\nSPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED=false \\\\\\nTZ=UTC \\\\\\n  \\\"$MS\\\" > \\\"$DATA_DIR/mothership.log\\\" 2>&1 &\\nMS_PID=$!\\n\\nok=\\\"\\\"\\nfor _ in $(seq 1 50); do\\n  if resp=$(curl -s --max-time 2 \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/healthz\\\" 2>/dev/null) && \\\\\\n     [ \\\"$(printf '%s' \\\"$resp\\\" | jq -r '.status // empty' 2>/dev/null)\\\" = \\\"ok\\\" ]; then\\n    ok=1; break\\n  fi\\n  sleep 0.3\\ndone\\n[ -n \\\"$ok\\\" ] || { log \\\"mothership never became healthy\\\"; tail -30 \\\"$DATA_DIR/mothership.log\\\"; exit 1; }\\nlog \\\"mothership healthy\\\"\\n\\n# --- Apply the fixture BEFORE starting the sim (bf-gdfwx fix; mirrors ----------\\n# run-sim-ble-match.sh's proven seed_fixture). Register the person + bind the\\n# sim's advertised device addr while the mothership is up but the sim is not yet\\n# streaming, so the device is bound standalone and the binding is in place when\\n# the first --ble adv arrives.\\nseed_fixture() {\\n  curl -s -X POST \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/ble/devices/preregister\\\" \\\\\\n    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\\\\n    -d \\\"{\\\\\\\"mac\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"$WALKER_MAC\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"label\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\"}\\\" >/dev/null\\n  person_id=$(curl -s -X POST \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/people\\\" \\\\\\n    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\\\\n    -d \\\"{\\\\\\\"name\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"color\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"$PERSON_COLOR\\\\\\\"}\\\" \\\\\\n    | jq -r '.id // empty' 2>/dev/null)\\n  [ -n \\\"$person_id\\\" ] || die \\\"could not create person \\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\" via /api/people\\\"\\n  curl -s -X PUT \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/ble/devices/$WALKER_MAC\\\" \\\\\\n    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\\\\n    -d \\\"{\\\\\\\"person_id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"$person_id\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"label\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\"}\\\" >/dev/null\\n  SEED_PERSON_ID=\\\"$person_id\\\"\\n\\n  # Sanity: confirm the binding took (reuses the child-1 gate).\\n  rec=$(curl -s --max-time 2 \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/ble/devices?registered=true\\\" \\\\\\n    | jq -c --arg mac \\\"$WALKER_MAC\\\" '[.devices[]|select(.mac==$mac)][0]' 2>/dev/null)\\n  pid=$(printf '%s' \\\"$rec\\\" | jq -r '.person_id // empty' 2>/dev/null)\\n  [ -n \\\"$pid\\\" ] || { log \\\"fixture FAILED: $WALKER_MAC not bound (child-1 regression)\\\"; exit 1; }\\n}\\n\\nlog \\\"applying fixture (register \\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\" + bind $WALKER_MAC) BEFORE sim...\\\"\\nSEED_PERSON_ID=\\\"\\\"\\nseed_fixture\\nlog \\\"fixture bound: $WALKER_MAC -> \\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\" (id=$SEED_PERSON_ID)\\\"\\n\\n# --- Start sim WITH --ble (the fixture precondition run-sim-local.sh omits) ------\\n\\\"$SIM\\\" \\\\\\n  --mothership \\\"ws://localhost:$PORT/ws/node\\\" \\\\\\n  --nodes \\\"$SIM_NODES\\\" --walkers \\\"$SIM_WALKERS\\\" --rate \\\"$SIM_RATE\\\" \\\\\\n  --space \\\"$SIM_SPACE\\\" \\\\\\n  --duration \\\"$SIM_DURATION\\\" --seed \\\"$SIM_SEED\\\" --ble \\\\\\n  > \\\"$DATA_DIR/sim.log\\\" 2>&1 &\\nSIM_PID=$!\\nlog \\\"sim started (pid $SIM_PID, --ble enabled)\\\"\\n\\n# --- Observe /api/blobs for non-empty canonical identity over the run window ----\\n# Identity is written by the Stage 2b write-back each fusion tick; the matcher\\n# triangulates from cached RSSI (~5s BLE cadence). Poll at 0.5s cadence over the\\n# full window (real t=0..duration) so the narrow early blob window is not missed.\\n# Capture the first blob that carries identityResolved==true OR personName != \\\"\\\"\\n# as the canonical evidence; also snapshot /api/ble/matches as corroboration.\\npeak_blobs=0\\nidentity_blob=\\\"\\\"        # first /api/blobs blob JSON with non-empty personName\\nidentity_blob_t=\\\"\\\"\\nmatch_json=\\\"\\\"\\nsample_count=0\\nticks=$(( SIM_DURATION * 2 ))    # 0.5s cadence\\nfor i in $(seq 1 \\\"$ticks\\\"); do\\n  sleep 0.5\\n  sample_count=$((sample_count+1))\\n  t_ms=$(( i * 500 ))\\n  raw=$(curl -s --max-time 2 \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/blobs\\\" 2>/dev/null)\\n  n=$(printf '%s' \\\"$raw\\\" | jq 'length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)\\n  [ \\\"$n\\\" -gt \\\"$peak_blobs\\\" ] && peak_blobs=$n\\n\\n  # identityResolved==true OR personName != \\\"\\\"  => resolved canonical identity\\n  id_blob=$(printf '%s' \\\"$raw\\\" | jq -c \\\\\\n    '[.[] | select((.identityResolved==true) or (.personName // \\\"\\\" | length > 0))][0]' 2>/dev/null)\\n  if [ \\\"$id_blob\\\" != \\\"null\\\" ] && [ -n \\\"$id_blob\\\" ]; then\\n    identity_blob=\\\"$id_blob\\\"\\n    [ -z \\\"$identity_blob_t\\\" ] && identity_blob_t=\\\"${t_ms}ms\\\"\\n    [ -z \\\"$match_json\\\" ] && match_json=$(curl -s --max-time 2 \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/ble/matches\\\" 2>/dev/null)\\n    break\\n  fi\\n\\n  if [ $(( i % 2 )) -eq 0 ]; then\\n    resolved=$(printf '%s' \\\"$raw\\\" | jq '[.[]|select(.identityResolved==true)]|length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)\\n    printf '  t=%2ds  blobs=%s  peak=%s  identityResolved_blobs=%s\\\\n' \\\"$((i/2))\\\" \\\"$n\\\" \\\"$peak_blobs\\\" \\\"$resolved\\\"\\n  fi\\ndone\\n\\n# Stop the sim so the run is bounded, then tally diagnostics.\\nkill \\\"$SIM_PID\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\nwait \\\"$SIM_PID\\\" 2>/dev/null; SIM_EXIT=$?\\nSIM_PID=\\\"\\\"\\nreject_count=$(grep -ciE 'reject|invalid_token|\\\\b401\\\\b|\\\\b403\\\\b' \\\"$DATA_DIR/sim.log\\\" 2>/dev/null)\\n\\n# If identity was observed mid-run, take one more stable sample (matcher persists\\n# after sim stop via identity persistence) for a clean evidence capture.\\nif [ -n \\\"$identity_blob\\\" ] && [ \\\"$identity_blob\\\" != \\\"null\\\" ]; then\\n  raw=$(curl -s --max-time 2 \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/blobs\\\" 2>/dev/null)\\n  fresh=$(printf '%s' \\\"$raw\\\" | jq -c \\\\\\n    '[.[] | select((.identityResolved==true) or (.personName // \\\"\\\" | length > 0))][0]' 2>/dev/null)\\n  [ \\\"$fresh\\\" != \\\"null\\\" ] && [ -n \\\"$fresh\\\" ] && identity_blob=\\\"$fresh\\\"\\nfi\\n# Final matcher surface snapshot (corroboration).\\nfinal_matches=$(curl -s --max-time 2 \\\"http://localhost:$PORT/api/ble/matches\\\" 2>/dev/null)\\nfinal_summary=$(printf '%s' \\\"$final_matches\\\" \\\\\\n  | jq -c '[.[] | {blob_id, person_name, device_addr, confidence, is_ble_only}]' 2>/dev/null)\\n\\necho \\\"\\\"\\nlog \\\"---- RESULT ----\\\"\\nlog \\\"seeded person: \\\\\\\"$PERSON_NAME\\\\\\\" (id=$SEED_PERSON_ID)  device: $WALKER_MAC\\\"\\nlog \\\"peak_blob_count=$peak_blobs\\\"\\nlog \\\"identity_blob_at=${identity_blob_t:-never}\\\"\\nlog \\\"samples=$sample_count (0.5s cadence)  nodes=$SIM_NODES walkers=$SIM_WALKERS rate=$SIM_RATE duration=${SIM_DURATION}s seed=$SIM_SEED ble=on\\\"\\nlog \\\"sim_exit=$SIM_EXIT  reject_in_sim_log=${reject_count:-0}\\\"\\necho \\\"\\\"\\nlog \\\"evidence: /api/blobs blob with non-empty canonical identity:\\\"\\nif [ -n \\\"$identity_blob\\\" ] && [ \\\"$identity_blob\\\" != \\\"null\\\" ]; then\\n  printf '%s\\\\n' \\\"$final_summary\\\" | head -40\\nelse\\n  log \\\"  (none observed)\\\"\\nfi\\necho \\\"\\\"\\n# Persist the raw evidence for the note / audit.\\nprintf '%s\\\\n' \\\"$identity_blob\\\" > \\\"$DATA_DIR/identity_blob.json\\\"\\nlog \\\"mothership matcher-related log lines (INFO/WARN):\\\"\\ngrep -iE 'ble:|identity|triangulat|match' \\\"$DATA_DIR/mothership.log\\\" 2>/dev/null | tail -15 || log \\\"  (none)\\\"\\n\\n# --- Acceptance gate: a live blob carried non-empty canonical identity ----------\\nif [ -z \\\"$identity_blob\\\" ] || [ \\\"$identity_blob\\\" = \\\"null\\\" ]; {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_197bacb21709429eb22d2b6e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\n3\tdescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\n4\tmetadata: \n5\t  node_type: memory\n6\t  type: reference\n7\t  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\tIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n11\t\n12\t**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n13\t\n14\t**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n15\t1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n16\t2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n17\t3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n18\t   ```python\n19\t   import sqlite3, re\n20\t   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n21\t   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n22\t   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n23\t   con.execute('BEGIN')\n24\t   for rid, ts in rows:\n25\t       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n26\t           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n27\t                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n28\t   con.commit()\n29\t   ```\n30\t   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n31\t4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\n32\tNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n33\t\n34\t**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). A `sqlite3` binary was available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3` as of 2026-07-06 (not on PATH) — but **as of 2026-07-08 that Nix store path is GONE** (`find /nix/store -name sqlite3` returns nothing; it was garbage-collected). **`python3`'s built-in `sqlite3` module is now the primary repair method, not a fallback** — there is no sqlite3 binary on this box. One-liner scoped repair via python3:\n35\t```bash\n36\tpython3 -c \"import sqlite3; c=sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db'); n=c.execute(\\\"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=REPLACE(claimed_at,' ','T')||'Z' WHERE bead_id='bf-XXXX' AND length(claimed_at)=19\\\").rowcount; c.commit(); print('repaired',n)\"\n37\t```\n38\t\n39\t**Confirmed again 2026-07-07** (bf-601bz: 4 length-19 rows blocked close; scoped the WHERE to `bead_id='<id>'` for a targeted fix). Both RFC3339 suffixes are accepted by `br close`: the `+00:00` form above AND trailing `Z` via `strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', claimed_at)` (equivalently `REPLACE(ts,' ','T')||'Z'`) both worked. Remember to `br sync --flush-only` after closing so the checkpoint persists.\n40\t\n41\t**Confirmed again 2026-07-08** (bf-14wx5: 4 length-19 rows from today's dispatches blocked close; scoped `WHERE bead_id='bf-14wx5'` repair to `Z`-suffix via python3 sqlite3 — no sqlite3 binary present). Same close hit surfaced during an [[auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads]] resolution (branch b: implemented-but-uncommitted → commit + close).\n42\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"319a3fff-8392-4de6-9a96-11b47e681bbd","uuid":"80eabc0d-da1d-41ad-b9bc-c7d75f4cee37","timestamp":"2026-07-08T20:17:07.720Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339.md","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). A `sqlite3` binary was available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3` as of 2026-07-06 (not on PATH) — but **as of 2026-07-08 that Nix store path is GONE** (`find /nix/store -name sqlite3` returns nothing; it was garbage-collected). **`python3`'s built-in `sqlite3` module is now the primary repair method, not a fallback** — there is no sqlite3 binary on this box. One-liner scoped repair via python3:\n```bash\npython3 -c \"import sqlite3; c=sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db'); n=c.execute(\\\"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=REPLACE(claimed_at,' ','T')||'Z' WHERE bead_id='bf-XXXX' AND length(claimed_at)=19\\\").rowcount; c.commit(); print('repaired',n)\"\n```\n\n**Confirmed again 2026-07-07** (bf-601bz: 4 length-19 rows blocked close; scoped the WHERE to `bead_id='<id>'` for a targeted fix). Both RFC3339 suffixes are accepted by `br close`: the `+00:00` form above AND trailing `Z` via `strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', claimed_at)` (equivalently `REPLACE(ts,' ','T')||'Z'`) both worked. Remember to `br sync --flush-only` after closing so the checkpoint persists.\n\n**Confirmed again 2026-07-08** (bf-14wx5: 4 length-19 rows from today's dispatches blocked close; scoped `WHERE bead_id='bf-14wx5'` repair to `Z`-suffix via python3 sqlite3 — no sqlite3 binary present). Same close hit surfaced during an [[auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads]] resolution (branch b: implemented-but-uncommitted → commit + close).\n","numLines":42,"startLine":1,"totalLines":42}}}
